title: Modern Clients, Modern Problems
excerpt:
Dear Coaches, Therapists, Counsellors, Psychotherapists, and whoever this resonates with:
Does your website pass the 3-step test? Most don't, and it’s costing you more clients than you think.
body:
Who are your clients? Who are you trying to provide a service for? What is your niche? Unfortunately, this matters more than you think when it comes to YOU and your online presence.

We live in a world where our clients WILL put our name into Google, ChatGPT, Gemini, and other search engines. I do it, YOU do it, and your clients do it. There is no question about that part.

If we can agree on all of that, then please tell me: why does your website look the way it does? Yes, YOUR website specifically. I search and scroll through hundreds of sites each week as a hobby, and it is extremely rare to find a practice that has actually paid the slightest bit of attention to the way they show up. And no - having a website is not better than not having one.

“Reality check”
A rubbish site will actively turn your clients away. If you own one and you are so proud of it because you have done it all by yourself, please make sure you ask for an opinion from the right people, not your inner circle.

The website is the core, the absolute centre of your practice. It can be everything you wish and want: the main client route to find you, the contract generator, the form collector, the practice booking system, the payment collection method, the reminders, and even a room booking system (yes, I can do that)... the list is endless, really. But your site isn't doing any of that, is it? Instead, so many people - likely yourself included - end up paying for directories just to be included in a never-ending list and somehow stay visible because their website has become an afterthought.

LET’S CHANGE THIS. Let’s make our sites presentable, professional, unique, and beautiful, and most importantly, let’s attract the right people! All of that is easier than you think, and crazy enough, it’s all possible for FREE without any monthly subscriptions, retainers, or anything like that.

By having a truly presentable website that works and does what’s intended, you show that your clients matter, that they are not an afterthought, and it gives them many ways they can connect to you and be part of the journey. A website isn’t just for you; it is for them, too.

“Are you proud to give your website as a gift to your client? If not, there is work to be done.”

Especially if your niche is ADHD, LGBT, Depression, or Anxiety... more often than not, your website works as a deterrent.

Take my advice: you don’t have to do it with me, but please DO look into it, as let’s pay attention to our online presence a little bit more.

The "Is My Website Helping?" Checklist
Be honest. Look at your site and answer these three questions:

1. Does it load under 3 seconds?
- Fact: Over 70% of potential clients will bounce if your site doesn't load instantly.
2. Is it clear who you help? (In under 5 seconds?)
- Fact: Users form an opinion about your website in 0.05 seconds; if they don't know what you do immediately, they leave.
3. Is it easy to book you? (Right now?)
 - Fact: Every extra click between a client and your booking calendar reduces the chance of them actually booking by 20%.

If you answered "No" to any of these, or aren't sure - we need to talk.

I’m offering you a free 30-minute conversation to audit your online presence. No strings, just a professional look at your site to see how we can turn your digital space into a tool that actually works for you and your clients.

title: Write it, ReWrite it, and Make it happen
excerpt: 3 unique and proven steps to grow a successful business by expanding our own beliefs and noticing new perspective.
body:
## Our mind is very efficient, **however** sometimes... a little **too** efficient

It tends to compare and **stop us** before we even think of the **first step** . Thoughts like... "But it already exists," "There are others doing this," "But they have better credentials," "They are world champions in this, how can I compare?" Really, just a **never-ending loop** of thoughts as to why it is not worth doing.

I am sure you have heard this kind of sentence from someone or you might even be the **"someone."**

**I can relate...** I definitely felt like that before in my life and **I get it...** It is so hard not to feel that way. To feel like the least qualified person for a thing that for a split second felt like the best thing ever. Well... **All is not lost.** I have learned, designed, and successfully implemented these **3 key** steps to overcome this and grow in ways you can't even imagine... *(for now)*.

## **First** step – ** *Write* ** it down
Yup, **dead simple...** write it down, but not just the *"why not"* part... Write the **whole sentence** down. That way you can see it clearly and close the loop. It's like having a part of your favourite song on repeat in your head and **it... just... won't... stop...** no matter what. **The trick?** You must listen to the **end of the song** and that's exactly what we need to do with this loop. By writing it down, we can finally let it go from our mind and **YOU** end up with your current core belief sentence as to *"why not you."* It might sounds something like, "I don't feel like it's going to work for me as there are others that are qualified in a better place (mention the actual place) or hold better certificates (yup, details, details, details) or they are world champions…" Just keep going, keep writing it down however long it might be, and more importantly, try to be as **specific** as possible.

## **Second** step – Turn the sentence on its * **head** * 

Every little part about **why not...** take it and think of **ways** and **whys** and **hows** that might be a reason for people **not to** get in touch with them, with this *ideal person* you have written about in Step 1. Seems simple, but **try it** first and see that creativity and brainstorming are required to be on **overdrive** here. It might sound like, "Yes he/she is a champion but some people might be intimidated by the level of professionalism, they might charge too much, no point to even look, they only work with the best clients, who am I to get lessons from world champions..." and so on... thanks to this exercise you will be able to **shift your core beliefs** and mindset on the topic.

**Word of advice on this...** in the world of *AI*, it is very easy to just ask AI all these questions and it will *throw a page* of text back at you... however, that is **USELESS** as this needs to **come from you**, from your **mind**, and your **beliefs**. This is an exercise on **changing beliefs**... not reading pages and pages of garbage information.

## And finally, the **third** step – Can you can ** *see it* ** ?

Go ahead and apply **yourself**, your **uniqueness**, and your **personal touch** into the space created by the previous exercise. By this point, you have a somewhat **good understanding** and you are able to see that existing options are **not as perfect** as they seemed before. I am sure you can find many ways in which you can apply your **perspective, experience, and expertise.** Please go ahead and **claim some of the space for yourself.** The final writing exercise is all about **you** and your angle. Go ahead and use **your** creativity and freedom to write all the ways **you are the best person to get in touch with**. Search for all the unique and **make... it... happen!**

**Imagine** how many amazing people are **stuck** at the first step, not finishing the loop of thought and having these *"why not me"* messages flooding their minds... Thanks to **these steps**, you are now ahead of **millions of people** who sadly won't take the first step needed.

### Bonus point

Having all this knowledge, **take your time** and remember that you are **not restricted or restrained**; you are **free**, free to imagine, free to come up with, and free to write the **"WHY ME"** sentence however you like... Free to imagine why a client would choose you and why you, your skills, and your unique perspective **will make you successful** and follow through!

## Conclusion

We **worry so much** about other people and how they are better. We end up **forgetting** to think and reflect on what makes **us unique** and why we would be chosen. These are not only **3 simple steps**; it's a reframing technique to find your **confidence** to do what you have considered impossible. And most importantly, **to succeed**.

title: ADHD and the Motivation Myth
excerpt: Neurotypical productivity advice is built on assumptions that don't apply to ADHD brains. Here's what actually works.
body:
## Built for a Different Brain

Most productivity systems were designed by and for neurotypical minds. They assume consistent motivation, linear task completion, and the ability to start on demand.

For ADHD brains, **none of these assumptions hold**.

## The ADHD Motivation System

Neurotypical motivation responds to importance and deadlines. ADHD motivation responds to:

- **Interest** Is this genuinely engaging right now?
- **Challenge** Is there a level of difficulty that creates flow?
- **Novelty** Is this new enough to activate dopamine?
- **Urgency** Is there a real, immediate consequence?

This isn't a character flaw. It's a neurological reality.

## Systems That Work With Your Brain

**External structure over internal willpower.** Use body doubling, accountability partners, visible timers, and environmental design.

**Work with your interest cycles.** When something is engaging, go deep. Don't fight the hyperfocus channel it.

**Reduce friction to zero.** The fewer decisions required to start, the more likely you are to begin.

**Celebrate completion, not perfection.** ADHD brains are acutely sensitive to failure signals. Build in wins.

## ADHD-Aware Coaching

I work with a number of clients who are navigating life and ambition with ADHD. The approach is different and the results, when it's tailored correctly, are remarkable.

If this resonates, I'd love to have a conversation.

title: Identity Before Habits
excerpt: You don't rise to your goals you fall to your identity. Until you shift how you see yourself, habits are just performances.
body:
## The Identity Gap

Most habit advice focuses on behaviour. Do this. Track that. Build a streak.

It misses the deeper layer: **who you believe yourself to be**.

## The Athlete Principle

Ask someone who exercises consistently why they do it. They won't say "because of my habit tracker." They'll say some version of: *I'm someone who moves their body.*

The behaviour flows from the identity. Not the other way around.

## How to Shift Identity

Identity shifts come through evidence. Small, consistent actions that prove to yourself you are the person you want to become.

- You don't *try* to read more you become someone who reads
- You don't *try* to eat well you become someone who takes their health seriously
- You don't *try* to be productive you become someone who follows through

The coaching work is in accelerating this identity evolution removing the counterevidence (the exceptions, the excuses) and stacking the proof.

## The Coaching Conversation

In my practice, the first sessions are rarely about habits. They're about unpacking the story you're telling about who you are and what you're capable of.

That's where the real work begins.

title: The Longevity Basics Nobody Told You About
excerpt: Before expensive supplements and biohacking gadgets here are the five evidence-based fundamentals that actually move the needle.
body:
## Evidence Over Hype

The longevity industry is worth billions. Most of it is noise.

Before you spend money on NAD+ infusions or continuous glucose monitors, there are **five fundamentals** that the research consistently shows matter most. They're unglamorous. They work.

## The Five Fundamentals

**1. Sleep quality over quantity**
Seven to nine hours is the target, but *architecture* matters as much as duration. Consistent sleep and wake times regulate your circadian rhythm more than anything else.

**2. Zone 2 cardio — 150 minutes per week**
This is the single most evidence-backed longevity intervention available. Low intensity, long duration aerobic exercise trains your mitochondria and metabolic efficiency.

**3. Protein at every meal**
Muscle mass is one of the strongest predictors of healthy ageing. Most people eat far too little protein, especially as they age.

**4. Strength training twice per week minimum**
Not optional. Muscle is metabolically active tissue. Sarcopenia (age-related muscle loss) accelerates every other ageing marker.

**5. Stress regulation**
Chronic cortisol elevation is profoundly ageing. This isn't about elimination it's about recovery. Sleep, nature, relationships, breathwork.

## Where Coaching Fits

Most people know *what* to do. The gap is in the doing consistently, sustainably, year after year.

That's where a longevity coach earns their place.

title: Why Smart People Keep Starting Over and How to Finally Stop
excerpt: The restart loop isn't a discipline problem. It's an architecture problem. Understanding the real mechanics behind why driven people self-sabotage is the first step to building something that actually holds.
body:
## The Pattern Nobody Talks About

You've been here before. A new week, a new plan, a fresh burst of motivation. This time it's going to be different.

Then somewhere between day three and day ten, the momentum quietly disappears.

This isn't a willpower failure. **It's a systems failure** and there's a fundamental difference.

## Why High Achievers Are Most Vulnerable

Counterintuitively, it's often the most capable, intelligent people who get stuck in the restart loop longest. Here's why:

- They can sustain effort through sheer force of will temporarily
- They learn to confuse intensity with progress
- They haven't yet built systems that work *without* motivation

The restart loop is seductive precisely because the starts feel so good. New beginnings carry natural energy.

## The Architecture Fix

What you need isn't more discipline. You need better architecture systems that function on your lowest-energy day, not just your best one.

**Start smaller than feels meaningful.** The goal isn't the action, it's the identity reinforcement that comes from showing up.

Build your environment before you need your willpower. Make the right choice the easy choice.

## Working With a Coach

This is exactly what I work on with clients in the *Break the Loop* programme. We identify your specific restart triggers, rebuild your systems from the ground up, and create accountability structures that outlast motivation.

If you're ready to stop starting over, I'd love to talk.
